4th-make Shemhazai<tab>0<tab>A IVth Legion Zealot transformed by the power of auracite into a twisted monstrosity resembling Shemhazai, the Whisperer of Ivalician legend. She is believed to hold sway over the spirits of the netherworld.

4th-make Cuchulainn<tab>0<tab>This former Zealot of the IVth Legion was reborn by the power of auracite in the likeness of Cuchulainn, the Impure of Ivalician legend. While he shares his name with a renowned voidsent, this is believed to be no more than coincidence.

G-Warrior<tab>0<tab>Originally created by the Allagans as a prototype for the Ultima Weapon, this warmachina was known as the Ultima Warrior before being restored and extensively modified by Garlond Ironworks. It was later called into action in the battle with the Sapphire Weapon, where its vast range of armaments were the deciding factor in overcoming its far larger opponent. Rumors suggesting the “G” stands for “gun” remain unverified, especially considering its weapon of choice is an aethersaber.

Алмазное Оружие<tab>0<tab>Завершающий экземпляр в серии Оружия VII легиона. Он вобрал в себя данные, собранные предыдущими машинами в боях с Воином Света, и был оснащён новейшей системой «Над-разум». Если бы Гарлеанской Империи удалось обрушить разрушительную мощь Алмазного оружия на Эорзию, последствия были бы катастрофическими.

Diablo Armament<tab>0<tab>Hidden deep within the ruins below Zadnor, it was recently unearthed by Menenius of the IVth Imperial Legion. Evidence indicates that it is a voidsent which has undergone aetherochemical enhancement, in order to participate in the subjugation of the southern continent.

2B<tab>0<tab>Официально «YoRHa номер 2, единица класса В», этот андроид в чёрном лишь внешне похож на 2P. На самом деле андроиды в белом и прочие машины — её смертельные враги, и она прилагает огромные усилия к их уничтожению. Её, по-видимому, тесная связь с андроидом 9S не может не вызывать любопытства...

Troll<tab>0<tab>An Aerslaent fiend of towering height, a troll's upper body is covered in bristly hairs, while its lower half is exposed skin with the hardened appearance of pinecone. To the Roegadyn of its home island, this beast is seen as a rite of passage, for those who can defeat one are granted license to board the longships.

Pisaca<tab>0<tab>A vicious Thavnairian scalekin with plating as strong as steel, claws as sharp as swords, and a tongue as long and lacerating as a whip. Its savagery is such that it rarely hesitates to attack even armed travelers. Little wonder, then, that local legends cast it as a fierce demon.

Ea<tab>0<tab>An ancient race of sentient life-forms which Meteion reanimated within Ultima Thule. So advanced was their civilization, they were able to unlock the secret to immortality. However, their discovery of the universe's inevitable destruction saw them sink into a despair so unending and dark, they prayed for deliverance through extinction.

Arkasodara<tab>0<tab>A faction of Matanga whose ancestors were indigenous to Thavnair. Following the appearance of the Au Ra, they joined forces with these new arrivals to drive the rival Gajasura tribe from their lands. Later, Hyuran settlers would come to the island's shores, and together did these disparate peoples establish the city-state of Radz-at-Han.

Loporrit<tab>0<tab>A race of creatures resembling rabbits and living in Mare Lamentorum. In truth, they are arcane entities assigned by Hydaelyn to crew the star's great ark─the moon. For centuries have they labored in secret, exchanging clandestine communications with the Sharlayans and patiently preparing for the coming of the Final Days.

Argos<tab>0<tab>A familiar crafted by Venat the ancient. At first blush, it appears to be a canine covered in flaxen fur, but in reality it is an entity composed almost exclusively of pure aether. Following the sundering, this creature has been employed to assist those who oversee the moon.

Hermes<tab>0<tab>Hermes, chief overseer of Elpis, in a transformed state. As a skilled crafter of avian creations, he is naturally well versed in wind-aspected magicks, and the herald's staff he carries allows him to commune with Meteion and her sisters as they travel the universe.

Amon the Undying<tab>0<tab>A fragment of the master technologist Amon's spirit, sunk deep within the aetherial sea. The soul was originally that of Hermes, before it was sundered by Hydaelyn and reborn countless times over the ages. Yet this lingering shard is no longer him, or even Fandaniel, but belongs wholly and utterly to Amon.

Erichthonios<tab>0<tab>The harrowing form taken by Erichthonios─warder of the condemned─when he set upon Themis and the Warrior of Light as they attempted to breach Pandæmonium in search of the truth. He is purportedly skilled in shackling magicks...to the exclusion of much else.

Magus Sisters<tab>0<tab>These false deities spawned by the Telophoroi were charged with guarding the Tower of Zot, the ominous spire which arose near the city-state of Radz-at-Han. They are the Manusya of Thavnairian myth made manifest─Sanduruva, Cinduruva, and Minduruva, three sisters who have been the object of worship since ancient times.

Meteion<tab>0<tab>A winged creation of Hermes's design capable of traversing the stars. As an entelechy, Meteion can use the power of dynamis to convert emotion into tangible phenomena. Long has she traveled the cosmos in search of the meaning of life, though her journey has taught her nothing save for despair.
Форшено Левейё<tab>0<tab>«Я уже знал о предсказанных Последних Днях. Я поклялся себе, что не позволю им украсть у вас будущее.»

Anima<tab>0<tab>A primal housed within the Tower of Babil, Anima was brought forth with the corpse of Varis zos Galvus and granted its unearthly power through the patriotism and fervor of the Empire's citizens. It sat at the center of the Telophoroi's web, siphoning rivers of aether through the spires built in every far-flung corner of the world.

Квинт ван Цинна<tab>0<tab>«Неравенство — вот корень всех раздоров, а мирные соглашения, построенные на компромиссах, мимолётны. Счастье для всех — это ребячество. В реальности же сильному достаётся всё.»
Альфино и Ализе (Endwalker)<tab>0<tab>«Мы встаём, падаем, и снова встаём!»
Гитлодей<tab>0<tab>«Нет, тебя ждёт собственное сражение. Там, где тебя никто не заменит.»

Vrtra<tab>0<tab>One of the seven great wyrms born of Midgardsormr, Vrtra took Thavnair as his home, where he lived in solitude for many centuries. This quietude was broken, however, when he brokered peace between warring tribes, and involved himself with the founding of Radz-at-Han. Since the city-state's establishment, he has ruled in secret alongside generations of satraps, guiding the people from behind his conspirators' mortal facade.

Zodiark<tab>0<tab>The eldest and most powerful of primals, created by the ancients to forestall the Final Days. Zodiark succeeded in His task of restoring balance to the star, and prevented the devastation which so many feared. Sundered by Hydaelyn's hand, He was never to reclaim His full and unbroken might.

Hydaelyn<tab>0<tab>This ancient primal was created under the leadership of Venat to curb the towering might of Zodiark. Summoned with a lesser sacrifice of souls, Hydaelyn chose to unleash a blow which sundered not only Zodiark but the star itself, a desperate act which allowed Her to imprison His shattered essence upon the moon. In appearance, She closely resembles Venat, who gave herself to serve as the primal's heart.
Зенос вий Гальв<tab>0<tab>«Ты <var 08 E905 ((нашла)) ((нашёл)) /var> в ней... удовлетворение?»

Rampart<tab>0<tab>Encountered in the undersea ruins of Alzadaal's Legacy, this floating construct seeks to discourage intruders by teleporting hostile ocean denizens into their midst. A product of Hannish alchemy and possessed of a glimmering crystal core, the rampart is more accurately classified as soulkin rather than forgekin.

Hippo Cart<tab>0<tab>Sturdy and reliable, the Hippo Riders' speedy carts will deliver anything to anywhere in two shakes of a hippo's tail. Gaze into their hippos' dark, watery eyes, and know that you're in good hands─and hooves! Offer includes a complimentary gulal shower, while supplies last. (This Triad card sponsored by: the Hippo Riders.)

Gajasura<tab>0<tab>This Matanga tribe was driven from its indigenous home of Thavnair when it refused to accept the peace made between the Arkasodara and newly immigrated Au Ra. Said to spread discord wherever they go, the Gajasura finally resettled on the Azim Steppe, where their loathing for the Au Ra festers to this day.

Ралгр<tab>0<tab>Ралгр — разрушитель миров и бог разрушения. Ралгр повелевает силой молний и часто изображается как маг, несущий бронзовый посох. Мощный физический облик, который он принимает в Аглае, как полагают, отражает визуализации его самых благочестивых монахов — Кулака Ралгра.

Azeyma<tab>0<tab>Keeper of the sun and goddess of inquiry, Azeyma is most often depicted as a beautiful noble lady holding a golden fan─and so does she appear in Aglaia. Though this manifestation welcomes curiosity as a patron of inquiry ought, she is also wont to test pilgrims with unbridled ferocity...

Nald<tab>0<tab>Overseer of the underworld, god of commerce, and guardian deity of Ul'dah, Nald is conventionally depicted alongside Thal as either his twin or a single god of dual aspect. In Aglaia, he appraises the value of pilgrims' souls as any good trader would.

Thal<tab>0<tab>Thal is but one aspect of the Traders who govern the underworld and commercial affairs. According to the teachings of the Order of Nald'thal, Nald presides over the world of the living, and Thal, the halls of the dead─and indeed, the twins' seamless transformations in Aglaia are strikingly reminiscent of the cycle of death and rebirth presented in the Order's liturgies.

Chi<tab>0<tab>A ruthless war machine developed on the Alphatron Star. Yet unsated after wreaking untold havoc across countless civilizations, it split from central command and independently sought out further targets. Although the key to the Omicrons' further development─and perhaps very survival─is thought to lie hidden within its memory, it is unclear what that might entail.

Daivadipa<tab>0<tab>Seeking the wisdom of man, this bestial god exchanged one of his three snakelike heads with a Manusya's─or so the original legend goes. As the only Daivadipa manifestation known to living memory was encountered near the Tower of Zot, it is likely that it─much like the recently sighted Magus Sisters─was merely a primal of the malevolent tower's making rather than the true Mrga.

Endsinger<tab>0<tab>Born of a multiplicity of messengers who sought hope amongst the stars, yet found naught but despair, the Endsinger long nested at the furthest edge of the heavens. Thence did she grant the universe one final song, lifted upon the winds of dynamis─the kindness of oblivion.
N-7000<tab>0<tab>«Данному агрегату трудно обработать предложение пушистой формы жизни... <врум>...»

Герион Стайер<tab>0<tab>Происходя из расы, использовавшейся в качестве подневольного труда в ушедшем Таналане, Герион странствовал по подземельям Эорзии, движимый стремлением основать собственную империю. Увы, в водных путях Силь'ди ему посчастливилось столкнуться с грозным искателем приключений, от руки которого его поиски закончились преждевременно.

Гладиатор Силь'ди<tab>0<tab>Обнаружённая в руинах Силь'ди, эта статуя воина имеет сходство со стражами храма Азимы. Сходство можно объяснить использованием белахдийской технологии при их строительстве, хотя эта конкретная единица, похоже, была создана для того, чтобы превосходно сражаться на мечах.

Тронный Рыцарь<tab>0<tab>Этот арканный солдат был создан во времена династии Торн для борьбы с чумой нежити. Из четырёх его рук две принадлежали павшему воину амал'джа, которые были добавлены, чтобы усилить его родство с огнём. Несмотря на его мамметоподобный вид, использование оккультизма при его создании означает, что он больше похож на голема, чем на механическую конструкцию.

Beatrice<tab>0<tab>An entity encountered in the Thirteenth, this high-ranking voidsent was mistress of a domain built on the memories of a once-existing castle. She is said to have led the cabal of eight priestesses who governed Troia, a nation since lost to the Darkness.

Byregot<tab>0<tab>The god of architecture and industry, Byregot commands the element of lightning, and is most often depicted as an ardent smith wielding a two-headed hammer. The muscular form he takes in Aglaia is a testament to faith in physical strength, a conviction common amongst the builders and laborers who worship him.

Shadowcaster Zeless Gah<tab>0<tab>An Amalj'aa warrior who died some two hundred thirty years ago. Skilled at warding magicks wielded by him alone, he voluntarily joined Ul'dah's efforts to purge the undead. Unfortunately, he was wounded in the course of protecting an ally, and subsequently turned into the very thing that he had sought to vanquish.

Scarmiglione<tab>0<tab>The archfiend of earth, and one of Golbez's circle of four. Scarmiglione consumed voidsent with gluttonous abandon, the conflicting energies giving rise to his hideous and misshapen appearance. Possessed of incredible regenerative properties, his deathless form seemed beyond defeat until Zero finally bound him in crystal.

Барбаричча<tab>0<tab>Архидемон ветра и один из четырёх кругов Голбеза, Барбаричча неистово гордилась своими струящимися локонами. Будь то оружие или броня, зачарованные пряди волос подчинялись каждой её мысли, и она использовала их в бою с яростной агрессией — темпераментом, сохранившимся со времен её земной жизни.
Главный ключник Лахабреа<tab>0<tab>«...Его слова были не более чем бредом истерзанного разума. Ненависть и отчаяние, которые он терпит, необратимо исказили его взгляд на мир.»

Hephaistos<tab>0<tab>Born from dark shades of Lahabrea's emotions and memory, Hephaistos was a mirror that the chief keyward could not bear to look upon. Although sealed away in Pandæmonium's deepest circle, he was liberated by an unknown force, and devoted himself to seeing Athena resurrected. By fusing his being with mythic creations, Hephaistos unlocked the hidden secrets of creation magicks, and his hands could mold the very concept of life itself.

Clockwork Onmyoji & Clockwork Yojimbo<tab>0<tab>These clockwork contraptions were manufactured to satisfy a commission from the Eorzean Alliance, which had shown an interest in using Far Eastern automata in its training exercises. The Hingan artisans who designed them included inbuilt protection against reverse engineering, rigging them to explode if their cores were exposed without the proper administrative permissions.
Мечташка<tab>0<tab>«Я обещаю не сдаваться, пока не найду свою мечту! Даже если это будет последнее, что я сделаю...»
Доброжелательный Пушистик<tab>0<tab>«Ух, моя бедная спина... Боже, после этого испытания мне полагается здоровая порция жира.»
Анден<tab>0<tab>«Будь у меня руки, я бы как следует стукнул себя по голове, чтобы разогнать кровь. ...Или, вернее сказать, сок.»

Suprae-Lugae<tab>0<tab>Despite engineering himself into a twisted amalgamation of flesh and metal, this magitek inventor was bested by an adventurer in the Tower of Babil. Further misfortune left him as a head without a body─thus did he contrive to attach his gleaming skull to the hulking remains of Barnabas, his “beautiful little boy,” and maximize his offensive capabilities.

Gancanagh<tab>0<tab>This anthropoid mandragora modified by aetherochemical experimentation was cultivated to be a charismatic leader to its troops. Too charismatic, perhaps, as its enhanced intelligence inspired it to devise a revolution against its overlords, which led to its subsequent banishment to Eureka Orthos for purposes of “reeducation.”

Menphina<tab>0<tab>The keeper of the twin moons and the goddess of love. Menphina commands the element of ice, and is often depicted as a maid carrying a round skillet. The large hound which appeared at her side in the phantom realm was a manifestation of Dalamud─the lesser moon known in legend as Menphina's loyal hound.

Cagnazzo<tab>0<tab>The archfiend of water, and one of Golbez's circle of four, Cagnazzo was pugnacious and warlike. He released a tide of voidsent into Garlemald through a voidgate in Lapis Manalis, and was later defeated at the hands of Eorzea's champion.

Rubicante<tab>0<tab>The archfiend of fire, and one of Golbez's circle of four. This intimidating warrior would pursue his goals with zealous abandon, conjuring hellish flames from the ashes of his own suffering in a bid to vanquish his foes.
Венат<tab>0<tab>«А когда человечество найдёт силы противостоять отчаянию, мы оборвём песнь опустошения. А та, что её поёт, узнает — наше путешествие далеко от завершения. Это я обещаю.»

Okuri Chochin<tab>0<tab>Rokkon's chochin were once a single lantern that had acquired a spirit after many years of use─and been summarily discarded by its fearful master. It sat patiently in Gorai's collection for a time, but when wickedness pervaded the temple, it divided itself into a multitude of mononoke, the better to devour men's souls as recompense for its abandonment.

Gorai the Uncaged<tab>0<tab>The ruin of the man once known as Gorai, head monk of the venerated Shojo Temple. An avid collector, his attachment to the many rare treasures in his possession served only to stoke the flames of his avarice─until naught of the man he was remained.

Моко Неугомонный<tab>0<tab>Уважаемый генерал времён Кровавых лет, Моко прославился тем, что с непревзойдённым мастерством сразил бесчисленное множество вражеских командиров. Опасаясь преданности народа этому свирепому воину, его сюзерен счёл за благо обезглавить Моко его же собственным клинком, пока тот спал. Теперь его дух обитает в доспехах, которые он когда-то носил в бою, и ждёт возможности утолить жажду мести.

Octomammoth<tab>0<tab>This colossal sea monster made its way into the Isle of Haam via an underwater cave system, where it claimed the Aetherfont as its territory. After being defeated by an adventurer, it became clear that many of the unexplained incidents in local waters could be attributed to the octopus-like fiend.

Proto-Carbuncle<tab>0<tab>Confined deep within Pandæmonium, this creation was equipped with crystallized body parts which would theoretically enable it to store large amounts of dense aether. This change made the creature's temperament unpredictable and cursed it with an insatiable appetite, and it was summarily branded as a failure by Elpis's researchers.

Goldor<tab>0<tab>In the clamoring halls of the Celestium arena did this arcane warrior clad in gold make his villainous debut. Later revealed to be yet another disguise employed by Martyn, the gilded sorcerer unleashed an assortment of spells amplified by a special device during his bout.

Shishio<tab>0<tab>A mythical beast feared in Hingashi since the days of yore, this creature figures in over one hundred legends. While some believe it to be the king of the nue, others insist it is a manifestation of fear itself, born from the quavering hearts of men who have heard the cries that reverberate amongst the mountains of Shishu at night.
Темис<tab>0<tab>«И среди этих бесчисленных звёзд именно твоя пересекла мой путь. За это я буду вечно благодарен.»

Enenra<tab>0<tab>This tsukumogami arose from a pipe said to grant its owner joy or sorrow according to its whims─though the fates of those that once possessed it seem curiously amenable to either interpretation, depending on who tells the tale. Perhaps that is why it takes the form of smoke, shrouding that which mortal eyes are so desperate to see laid bare.

Golbez<tab>0<tab>Leader of the four archfiends, this enigmatic knight in black marshaled a great host of voidsent in a campaign of conquest in the Thirteenth. Once his supremacy was established, Golbez's focus turned to his true ambition: invading the aether-rich world of the Source.

Athena<tab>0<tab>Pandæmonium's first chief keyward, Athena used a man named Claudien as a vessel to house her memories, traversing eons and coming within an ilm of godhood. Luckily for the whole star, her ambitions were cut short before they could be realized.

Ketuduke<tab>0<tab>Legends of the south sea isles tell of whales that soar through the heavens. Shockmaw is spoken of in hushed whispers, but on the island of Aloalo a creature named “Ketuduke” was an object of reverence. Believed to be a divine messenger, Ketuduke would descend from the skies on a night when the winds howled and waters raged. The faithful would then climb atop its back and be delivered to paradise.

Lala<tab>0<tab>Encountered on Aloalo Island, the lala's wooden construction sets it apart from the stone frames of other golems. Derived from the esoteric mathematics that formed the basis of arcanima, the geometries carved into its surface enable complex and adaptive defensive measures.

Statice<tab>0<tab>This mischievous faerie was left behind by her masters, but even after their deaths, her aether did not dissipate. After countless years of solitude, Statice's once congenial personality warped into something much more wicked. Perhaps due to a sense of kinship with other lost things, she makes ingenious use of items collected from shipwrecks and other detritus that washes ashore.
ПуПу<tab>0<tab>«ПуПу... Я с... ПуПуПуПу... далёкой... ПуПу... планеты.»

Loquloqui<tab>0<tab>This statue and the shrine which houses it were built by the forgotten people of the south sea isles. It is presumed that the figure represents a teller of prophecy, but there are no records to illuminate the faith of its builders. Newer arrivals to Aloalo came to revere the figure, calling it “she who speaks and is spoken to”─or “Speaker,” as Kalika so succinctly translates.

Nophica<tab>0<tab>Tender of soils and harvests and goddess of abundance, Nophica is often depicted as a jubilant farmer holding a scythe of steel. She is greatly revered in Gridania, and the deity's more warlike incarnation famously graces a recruitment poster for the Order of the Twin Adder. Although mythological tales put her at odds with Halone, the Fury, the two seemed to bear each other no ill will in Euphrosyne.

Althyk<tab>0<tab>Althyk is the god of time and the surveyor of change and space. He commands the element of earth, and is most often depicted as an austere emperor wielding a mythril greataxe. Said to be the first of the Twelve, his presence in Euphrosyne is one characterized by quiet authority, though he seems much softened in dealings with his younger sister, Nymeia.

Nymeia<tab>0<tab>Nymeia, the watcher of celestial bodies and goddess of fate, stood at her brother Althyk's side in Euphrosyne to test the might of mortals. Often depicted as a weaver donning a white, silken veil, her name is appended to the Nymeia lily for the resemblance the flower's petals have to her pale adornment.

Halone<tab>0<tab>Depicted as a relentless warrioress armed with a bronze greatshield, Halone is the uncontested primary deity of the Ishgardian Orthodox Church. As the mover of glaciers and goddess of war, the Fury demonstrated her propensity for battle in Euphrosyne, joyfully wielding sword and spear in her challenge against mortal adventurers.

Thaliak<tab>0<tab>Thaliak, ruler of rivers and wisdom and god of knowledge, is most often depicted as a reserved scholar holding an ashen staff. The citizens of Sharlayan voted to adopt Thaliak as their guardian deity, and his appearance in Thaleia is said to closely resemble the grand statue they raised in his name.

Llymlaen<tab>0<tab>Llymlaen, watcher of the seas and goddess of navigation, is depicted as a strong fisherwoman wielding a long-bladed harpoon. Described in myth as Oschon's lover, the tale of the bathing Llymlaen hurling a sword at him for peeping is generally agreed to be the later fabrication of whimsical bards.

Oschon<tab>0<tab>Oschon is ruler of the mountains and god of wanderers and vagrants. Depicted as a carefree ranger wielding a bow of yew, the majestic peaks and howling winds of the world are said to be of his divine creation. Oschon is the explorer Deryk's true form, and it was he who brought mortals to the Omphalos to fulfill the Twelve's behest.

Durante<tab>0<tab>Glimpsed within a fragment of Golbez's past, this memoriate swordsman strove to save the world at the side of his black-armored companion. Yet it was not to be. In a final act of vengeance, a fallen enemy corrupted Golbez, and Durante was forced to raise a blade against his former friend.

Eulogia<tab>0<tab>An amalgam of the Twelve, Eulogia embodies the love and magnanimity the gods harbor towards mortals. This divine entity presents the Twelve's final challenge, delighting in the evidence of man's potential to thrive in a world divested of deities.

Asura<tab>0<tab>Threefold of visage and sixfold of arm, the belligerent Asura belongs to the pantheon of Manusya venerated by the people of Thavnair. Her dominion over war makes her unpopular with the commonfolk, yet she has a zealous adherent in the merchant Vanhudi─a man who equates the struggle to amass wealth with righteous battle.

Zeromus<tab>0<tab>Зеромус — порождение Бездны неисчислимой мощи, рождённое на луне Тринадцатой из остаточного эфира Зодиарка. Наполненный сущностью великого змия Аждайи и движимый желанием вернуться «домой», он выжидает во Тьме, накапливая силы, чтобы прорвать барьер между мирами.
Клайв Росфилд<tab>0<tab>«Почему бы не создать место, где люди могут <var 1A 02 /var>жить<var 1A 01 /var> по своим правилам?»
Гулул Джа Джа<tab>0<tab>«Невежество — путь к конфликту. Взаимопонимание — путь к дружбе.»
Отис<tab>0<tab>«Честью своей клянусь, вы не тронете мою королеву или граждан этих земель!»
Зораал Джа<tab>0<tab>«Победитель станет править, его путь да будет омыт кровью.»
Кахкива<tab>0<tab>«Куда бы тебя ни завели приключения, храни меня в своём сердце, ладно? Я буду рада побывать там. Увидеть места, до которых так и не добралась.»
Вук Ламат<tab>0<tab>«Я собираюсь стать Служителем Рассвета — и я защищу мой народ!»
Сфен<tab>0<tab>«Я носительница воспоминаний королевы Сфен Александрийской, и моё стремление защитить свой народ превосходит всё остальное!»
Олье<tab>0<tab>«Позвольте тепло поприветствовать вас в «Золотом Блюдце» семьи Мандервилль! Чем могу вам помочь?»

Король Элмер III<tab>0<tab>«Я — финальный рубеж, который должны преодолеть юные игроки, прежде чем бросить вызов лучшим мастерам Тройного трио. Получить место среди избранных они смогут, лишь превзойдя меня и мои изощрённые тактики.»
Называшка<tab>0<tab>«Верно! Это я, Называшка! Погляжу, вы все очнулись ото сна!»
Крайл (Dawntrail)<tab>0<tab>«Так что будьте спокойны. Спокойны, мама и папа.»
Нитовикве<tab>0<tab>«Пока мы живы, всегда можем построить новый поезд. Так что верьте! И хватит ныть.»
Юлий пир Норбан<tab>0<tab>«Поговорим позже. Сейчас нужно драться! Наконец-то, шанс отплатить вам за спасение!»
Зеро<tab>0<tab>«Не важно, где мы теперь окажемся или сколько пройдёт времени. Наши узы вечны. Я верю в это.»

Пелупелу<tab>0<tab>Известные в первую очередь как торговцы-коробейники, этот низкорослый народ родом из горного региона Уркопачи. Их можно увидеть путешествующими по всему Туралу вместе со своими верными альпаками. Их характерные маски, напоминающие клювы, выполнены в подражание легендарному туральскому видраалю — Валигарманде.

Альпака<tab>0<tab>Эти четвероногие звери родом из гор Турала. Исполненные любопытства, но трусливые, они известны тем, что изрыгают зловонные комки жвачки в тех, кто их напугал. Говорят, что дикие альпаки, обитающие в долинах, — особенно настороженный вид.

Моблин<tab>0<tab>Обитая в густых лесах Козамауки, этот эксцентричный клан искусных шахтёров и собирателей имеет общее происхождение с гоблинами. Они зарабатывают на жизнь, обеспечивая наёмных ремесленников — своих «Верных Горшкам» — обильными материалами для ремесла и создавая идеальные условия для создания прибыльных шедевров.

Ветконосец<tab>0<tab>Встречается в низинах Йак Тэла. Этот странный вид внешне напоминает нелетающую птицу. Однако при ближайшем рассмотрении оказывается, что это передвигающаяся семянка. Она подражает облику облачных существ, чтобы заманивать ничего не подозревающих хищников, убивая потенциальных охотников и питаясь их жизненными соками.

Рронек<tab>0<tab>Это неуклюжее травоядное обитает на травянистых равнинах Шак Турала. Хотя обычно оно передвигается медленно, разъярённый рронек становится неудержимой силой, сокрушая своей массивной тушей любые препятствия на пути. Считается, что яркие отметины на их мордах позволяют зверям узнавать друг друга в стаде.

Обгонщик<tab>0<tab>Эти машины общего назначения были разработаны, чтобы восполнить нехватку рабочей силы после того, как население Александрии было опустошено войнами и бедствиями. Они не только искусны в повседневных делах, таких как уборка и техническое обслуживание, но также могут составить серьёзную конкуренцию в различных карточных играх.

Сплюш<tab>0<tab>Этот странный представитель уолонов — существ, близких к троллям, — намеренно заглатывает слизней, чтобы «выращивать» их у себя в желудке. Хотя потеря питательных веществ вызывает частые приступы сонливости, это позволяет зверю, если он почувствует угрозу, отрыгнуть проглоченных гостей в качестве подкрепления в бою.
